title: cert.exe
text: cert.exe The cert.exe executable is used to certify Uniface applications and facilitates the creation of public/private key pairs. cert.exe {-key} | {-certify ResourceDir/UAR PrivateKey} Switches Option Description -key Creates a private/public key pairs. -certify ResourceDir/UAR PrivateKey Certifies the target Uniface application. You need to specify the Resource directory, or the UAR you want to certify, and the PrivateKey. Description Cert is created to protect your database connections, as well as the integrity of your application against malicious third parties. Note: It is available on Windows only. Generating Private/Public key pairs You can generate key pairs with the cert.exe -key option. You can also generate them with other tools, as long as they follow the specifications we have set. For more information about generating keys with OpenSSL, see How to Certify a Uniface Application . Certifying Uniface applications To certify an entire directory or a UAR, using your private key, simply specify them as shown in the table above. When using a private key generated by third party tools, make sure it follows the specifications we have set for those keys. When specifying a directory instead of a UAR, make sure that the directory follows the Uniface resource structure, as in the directory you specified in $RESOURCES_OUTPUT . You cannot specify a sub-folder. For more information, see Certifying Uniface Applications . Related Links Certifying Uniface Applications Certifying a deployed Uniface application can safeguard the privacy of your data. This functionality helps you to verify the validity of your application during runtime, making sure that your database connection settings cannot be used with unauthorized applications.
